subject: ? Anthropic said no to the Pentagon. OpenAI said yes
text:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8542ebd2-db5d-4cab-8df1-ac683eee3963/Gemini_Generated_Image_vi75ctvi75ctvi75.png?t=1772421067)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/aeb6034e-ad54-43eb-8d60-5fc1749df45a/In_partnership_with...__17_.png?t=1772135384)
Caption: 

Welcome, humans. 

Well, it's official (_sorta_): [OpenAI just "raised" $110B](https://openai.com/index/scaling-ai-for-everyone/). We put "raised" in quotes because much of that headline number is [letters of intent](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/open-ai-funding-round-amazon.html), not money in the bank. **Here's how it actually breaks down:**

* **Amazon**: $50B total, but only $15B is deployed upfront. The remaining $35B is [conditional](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/27/openai-funding-nvidia-amazon) on OpenAI hitting milestones (reportedly tied to its IPO or achieving AGI). Amazon also expanded its AWS deal by $100B over 8 years.

* **Nvidia**: $30B, but much of this arrives as [compute capacity commitments](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/27/openai-raises-110b-in-one-of-the-largest-private-funding-rounds-in-history/) (3GW of inference, 2GW of training on Vera Rubin chips), not liquid cash.

* **SoftBank**: $30B, described as a [letter of intent](https://x.com/ns123abc/status/2027057882387243267) that may or may not convert to actual capital by year's end.

So the real number? **Probably closer to $15B in immediate deployable cash**, with the rest gated behind milestones, hardware deliveries, and SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son's mood. The round values OpenAI at $730B pre-money ($840B post), up from $500B in October. _Supposedly it?s still open for more investors if ya wanna get in pre-IPO! _

Now, one part of the above caught our attention: the AGI clause with Amazon. Last week, we?heard a rumor that Microsoft and OpenAI's AGI deal might boil down to a surprisingly specific threshold: **50% of all productivity tasks that the current human workforce can perform, as judged by an independent panel.**

**Which makes you wonder: **where is the benchmark that's supposed to track these "workforce tasks"? Personally, we thought that benchmark might be [GDPVal](https://openai.com/index/gdpval/), OpenAI's own eval that measures AI performance on real work across 44 occupations. Interestingly, [GPT-5.2 has been sitting at 49.7% on the GDPVal leaderboard](https://evals.openai.com/gdpval/leaderboard) since last September._49.7%. The threshold is 50%. That's... close._

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4846b11f-8f09-48ac-abfb-50033b71c08c/Screenshot_2026-03-01_at_5.04.09_PM.png?t=1772413463)
Follow image link: (https://evals.openai.com/gdpval/leaderboard)
Caption: 

Could the upcoming GPT-5.4, [reportedly set to release this week or next](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/open-ai-funding-round-amazon.html), be the first model to cross 50%+ on GDPVal? Is that why it?s been delayed the few times that it has already? And if it does come out this week, could it surpassing 50% on GDPVal qualify as "AGI" under Microsoft and OpenAI's deal? And IF it does... _where's the independent committee that's supposed to make that call?_

We think it's about time these companies come forward with the benchmarks and panels responsible for deciding all this. Otherwise, we're all left to speculate.

**Here?s what happened in AI today: **

* The Pentagon blacklisted Anthropic, then OpenAI swooped in.

* NVIDIA is building a dedicated inference chip using Groq's tech.

* DeepSeek's new V4 model is set to drop on or before March 4.

* Jeff Bezos's Project Prometheus AI lab wand to rollup firms disrupted by AI.

Advertise in The Neuron here! (https://info.technologyadvice.com/advertise-with-the-neuron)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6a4266bb-f638-4a6b-9d48-8eafccfdebfb/image.png?t=1765839660)
Caption: 

# The Pentagon vs Anthropic vs ?OpenAI??

**DEEP DIVE: **[This whole mess, explained in depth across 8 juicy parts.](https://www.theneuron.ai/explainer-articles/the-pentagon-vs-anthropic-explained-the-week-ai-drew-a-line-in-the-sand-and-the-government-kicked-it-over/)

Last Thursday, Anthropic drew a line: Claude _cannot_ be used for mass surveillance of Americans, or for fully autonomous weapons. By Friday evening, President Trump [ordered every federal agency](https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/trump-bans-anthropic-government-use-rcna261055) to stop using the company's technology.

The Pentagon went further, [designating Anthropic a "supply-chain risk to national security"](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/27/anthropic-pentagon-supply-chain-risk-claude)... a label normally reserved for companies deemed foreign adversaries to the US, like Huawei. Anthropic has [vowed to challenge the designation in court](https://www.anthropic.com/news/statement-comments-secretary-war), calling it "retaliatory and unprecedented."

**The backstory: **Anthropic had a [$200M Pentagon contract](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/trump-anthropic-ai-pentagon.html), and Claude was the _only_ AI model on the military's classified networks. The Department of War wanted "all lawful use" access. Anthropic said fine, except for two things:

* **No mass surveillance**: Don't use Claude to collect Americans' geolocation, browsing, and financial data from data brokers.

* **No autonomous weapons**: Keep a human in the loop before anything fires.

**The DoW's "compromise"?** [According to Axios](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/27/anthropic-pentagon-supply-chain-risk-claude), the contract language they sent overnight included loopholes that would let those safeguards be overridden at will. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei [responded](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/trump-anthropic-ai-pentagon.html): "We cannot in good conscience accede to their request."

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4d9559be-448a-4f01-9522-74536b234385/IMG_1795__1_.png?t=1772413605)
Caption: Needless to say, the White House did NOT like this? 

**Now here?s the ultimate twist: **

* Hours after the government moved against Claude? 

* And after [300+ Google employees and 60+ OpenAI staffers signed an open letter](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/27/employees-at-google-and-openai-support-anthropics-pentagon-stand-in-open-letter/) urging their companies to hold the same line?

* And after [Sam Altman went on TV](https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/27/tech/openai-has-same-redlines-as-anthropic-in-any-deal-with-the-pentagon) and said it has the same redlines as Anthropic?  

?[OpenAI announced its own Pentagon deal](https://openai.com/index/our-agreement-with-the-department-of-war/). Now, Sam Altman claims this deal includes the same two red lines plus a third (no AI-powered social credit systems). 

But Altman's [announcement on X](https://x.com/sama/status/2027578580159631610) got community-noted _almost immediately; first of all, _government officials [contradicted his framing](https://x.com/sama/status/2027578580159631610), saying OpenAI will actually allow "all lawful purposes." Altman held [an AMA](https://x.com/sama/status/2027900042720498089) the next day; _if you want to read his answers to the specific concerns that got voiced, go check it out! _

**Why this matters:** [hours after the ban](https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/iran-strikes-2026/card/u-s-strikes-in-middle-east-use-anthropic-hours-after-trump-ban-ozNO0iClZpfpL7K7ElJ2), the US military proceeded to use Claude during air strikes on Iran. CENTCOM had it running intelligence assessments, target identification, and combat simulations. _So the gov using Claude in real combat scenarios is not a hypothetical scenario; it?s actively happening, right now. _

As MIT physicist Max Tegmark [put it](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/28/the-trap-anthropic-built-for-itself/): the AI industry spent years lobbying against regulation, promising to govern itself. Now ?we have less regulation on AI systems in America than on sandwiches.? _This was by design, as the AI industry has done the opposite of regulatory capture (where the winners in an industry lobby the government to regulate downwards so competitor upstarts can?t comply): they captured the government to avoid regulation entirely._

Every AI company will eventually face this question: when the government says "give us everything," do you comply or push back? We posit an interesting thought experiment: [_If a person can declare themselves a conscientious objector to being used by their country in war?. could a company not do the same? _](https://theneuron.ai/explainer-articles/the-pentagon-vs-anthropic-could-a-company-be-a-conscientious-objector)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/7cf99573-4593-460e-bb90-7154a8d863f4/image.png?t=1765839774)
Caption: 

**FROM OUR PARTNERS**

### The $4 Billion Problem Hiding in Every Fast-Food Location

[So brands like White Castle use Miso Robotics.](https://invest.misorobotics.com/?utm_source=email&utm_medium=paid-partnership&utm_campaign=partnership185-380_02-23_varc_unitb_33180545100_YJ4ZPRQDHV&_bhiiv=opp_caaa847d-d761-4e10-9155-71f1d6c3fa84_a2f2203a&bhcl_id=e803fa1a-3361-4951-a759-33fa13b9e56e_1358dcaa-ca62-4a3e-96cd-c5ff13ec4abf_6234f2ce-90fd-4539-b098-42f6c1e01492) Miso?s AI-powered restaurant kitchen robot works 2X faster than average fry cooks. That means shorter lines and up to [3X more profit per location.](https://invest.misorobotics.com/?utm_source=email&utm_medium=paid-partnership&utm_campaign=partnership185-380_02-23_varc_unitb_33180545100_YJ4ZPRQDHV&_bhiiv=opp_caaa847d-d761-4e10-9155-71f1d6c3fa84_a2f2203a&bhcl_id=e803fa1a-3361-4951-a759-33fa13b9e56e_1358dcaa-ca62-4a3e-96cd-c5ff13ec4abf_6234f2ce-90fd-4539-b098-42f6c1e01492)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9f01d906-b564-444f-a00a-4c4fae2b042e/image.png?t=1769038568)
Caption: 

# Prompt Tip of the Day 

Recently, somebody asked why we?re so focused on Anthropic/Claude in the Prompt Tip of the Day lately. It?s a good question! 

**Frankly, it's because no one else is innovating as much as Anthropic for **_**general AI use-cases right now**_**, and you?re probably behind if you're not learning how to use Anthropic?s tools **(_maybe Perplexity Cloud changes that; still TBD)._

I know that sounds dramatic, but neither Google or OpenAI are making as easy to use, practically useful, _truly _agentic tools for normies (_like us)_. _Where is OpenAI?s_[?answer to Cowork, their supposed Google Workspace competitor](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/openai-quietly-designed-rival-google-workspace-microsoft-office)_? We heard a rumor it could be coming in the next few weeks? but they?re sleeping on it. _

It feels increasingly like the trick to working with AI is **reducing the total number of prompts you need to make in the first place**, through _actual _automation. 

Things like plugins, and skills, and now scheduled automations build those automations into the system for you. _Worry less about prompting, and more about building systems._

So yeah, today?s prompt tip is this: time to learn how to effectively use skills! 

Two resources will help you do this: 

1. Anthropic?s newly released Skills explainer video (see below). 

2. This 32 page guide, which we wrote a TL;DR on here (_we recommend all of the above!) _

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6c35f36b-3a8f-4cad-bae1-460bd971975d/Screenshot_2026-02-27_at_11.37.33_AM.png?t=1772221070)
Follow image link: (https://youtu.be/bjdBVZa66oU?si=ainXS0MsMYiOwEeN)
Caption: 

**[Want more like this? Check out all of February?s Prompt Tips of the Day here.](https://www.theneuron.ai/ai-news-digests/the-neurons-prompt-tip-of-the-day-digest---february-2026/)**

**P.S: **_in that same spirit, should we retire the ?Prompt Tip of the Day? branding and rebrand this section something like ?AI Skill of the Day? so it?s more broadly applicable to what you ACTUALLY need to learn in 2026? Let us know! _

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/31dbfaac-0721-4c34-b8e5-557c4a7cb3ca/image.png?t=1765839671)
Caption: 

# Treats to Try 

_*Asterisk = from our partners (only the first one!). _[_Advertise to 650K readers here_](https://info.technologyadvice.com/advertise-with-the-neuron)_!_

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/dc210b90-ad7e-4ec1-90da-ddc0a9a4651a/Newsletters_Image_1920x1080__6___1_.png?t=1772140358)
Follow image link: (https://ref.wispr.ai/neuron)
Caption: 

1. ***Stop typing long prompts**. Dictate full context and [Wispr Flow](https://ref.wispr.ai/neuron) turns it into a clean, structured prompt you can paste into your AI assistant in seconds. 

2. [Auto-memory](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory) lets Claude Code remember your build commands, debugging patterns, code style preferences, and project architecture across sessions so you don't have to repeat yourself.

3. [Claude Import Memory](https://claude.com/import-memory) lets you switch to Claude from ChatGPT or other assistants without starting over; it imports your conversation history and preferences.

4. [Now I Get It!](https://nowigetit.us/) helps you upload any scientific PDF and get an interactive web page explaining it in plain language.

5. [Google AI Edge Gallery](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/google-ai-edge-gallery/id6749645337) runs AI models offline on your iPhone for chatting, image queries, transcription, and mini-games.

6. [Pixel](https://www.getpixel.ai/) auto-builds creatives, audiences, and campaigns across LinkedIn, Meta, Google, and X for you based on a simple description or webpage link. 

7. [Notra](https://www.usenotra.com/) turns your daily work (meetings, Slack, docs) into publish-ready content like LinkedIn posts and blog drafts.

8. [Orca Engine](https://www.orcaengine.ai/) lets you play, mod, and host Minecraft servers in your browser by chatting with AI to install mods, generate datapacks, and troubleshoot.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/abf3b639-ca99-484e-992a-9cf6d8687a54/image.png?t=1765839770)
Caption: 

# Around the Horn 

1. [NVIDIA](https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/nvidia-plans-new-chip-to-speed-ai-processing-shake-up-computing-market-51c9b86e) is building a dedicated inference chip using Groq's technology, debuting at GTC next month. OpenAI committed to 3GW of capacity as the chip's biggest customer.

2. [OpenAI ](https://www.theguardian.com/technology/ng-interactive/2026/feb/28/chatgpt-ai-chatbot-mental-health)was sued by Kate Fox after her husband's ChatGPT dependency led to psychosis and suicide.

3. A consequence of the Pentagon vs Claude vs OpenAI saga: [Claude beat ChatGPT](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/01/anthropic-claude-chatgpt-app-downloads-pentagon) in US App Store downloads after the Pentagon blacklisted Anthropic. Katy Perry posted a heart over Claude's Pro plan. _Secretary Hegseth: Chief of Claude Marketing??_

4. [Goldman Sachs](https://www.theguardian.com/business/2026/mar/01/investment-ai-resistant-halo-companies-uk-eu-markets-goldman-sachs) flagged specific AI-resistant ?Halo? stocks driving UK / EU markets to record highs.

5. [Howard Marks](https://www.oaktreecapital.com/insights/memo/ai-hurtles-ahead) published a memo arguing AI has hit "Level 3" autonomous agents replacing knowledge work.

6. [DeepSeek](https://www.pymnts.com/artificial-intelligence-2/2026/deepseek-poised-to-unveil-latest-ai-model/)?s new V4 model is set to be released on or before March 4, timed to correspond with China?s ?Two Sessions? (_the country?s biggest annual political event). _

7. [China](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/28/why-chinas-humanoid-robot-industry-is-winning-the-early-market/) shipped 36x more humanoid robots than US competitors, explaining why it has won the early market.

8. [NFL staffers](https://www.nbcsports.com/nfl/profootballtalk/rumor-mill/news/fear-of-ai-eliminating-jobs-makes-its-way-to-football) voiced fears that AI could eliminate scouting and quality control jobs.

9. [Jeff Bezos's "Project Prometheus"](https://www.ft.com/content/7b1bdc9d-c857-4ec9-91b4-fb4f6dd2e43b) AI lab reportedly seeked tens of billions to acquire companies being disrupted by AI.
